channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Bringing the LLM Wiki Idea to a Codebase
item.description: Andrej Karpathy’s LLM Wiki idea is compelling because it treats knowledge as something that can be gradually ingested, organized, queried, and improved over time. That same idea applies...

item.title: I Built an HTTP 418 Teapot App from My Phone on a Road Trip (RFC 2324 Compliant)
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge What I Built HTTP 418 — I'm a Teapot is the definitive web experience for the most important HTTP status code ever written. In 1998, Larry...

item.title: Load Balancers and its strategies
item.description: Introduction A load balancer acts as an entry point for incoming requests, distributing traffic across multiple instances of an application, to ensure scalability, availability, and reliability....

item.title: Israeli strike kills infant girl in south Lebanon during father's funeral
item.description: Article URL: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israeli-strike-kills-infant-girl-south-lebanon-during-fathers-funeral-2026-04-12/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47744126...

item.title: Python Remove Window From Screen Share With Tkinter
item.description: here is code that hide the windows window from screen recording & screen share. please don't use it for interview cheating. import tkinter as tk from tkinter import Listbox, END import ctypes...

item.title: The agent over-applies everything: why “don’t” is my most-used word
item.description: Models: Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, Composer 1.5, Composer 2 In 60% of my agent sessions, I had to correct the output. The single most common word in those corrections was "DON'T". It was not "fix" or...

item.title: Real-Time Breath Detection in the Browser: Spectral Centroid, Dual-Path State Machines, and a Nasty iOS Bug
item.description: Microphone-based breath detection sounds simple until you actually try it. Energy goes up, energy goes down — that's a breath, right? In practice, you run into continuous breathing with no silence...

item.title: How to add cron job monitoring without changing your infrastructure
item.description: Most cron job monitoring advice involves significant infrastructure changes: configure a centralised logging stack, set up log forwarding, deploy a sidecar, integrate with your existing...

item.title: Auditing Claude Code: what I found and how I contained it
item.description: What Claude Code captures from your system (and how to contain it) In early March 2026, I noticed Claude Code behaving oddly with my shell environment. Sandbox settings weren't working as...

item.title: Specification-First Agentic Development: A Methodology for Structured, Traceable AI-Assisted Development
item.description: I like clean code. Most of the programs I've written over the years are reasonably well structured. Sure, there's always that moment every two years where I look at old code and think — evolved,...

item.title: Room for the Moon is thrillingly weird experimental pop
item.description: For obvious reasons, I've had Moon on the mind all week. So I was trying to figure out what I should recommend this week that would thematically fit. Brian Eno's Apollo: Atmospheres and...

item.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
item.description: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

item.title: Rockstar Games says hack will have ‘no impact’
item.description: Rockstar confirmed on Saturday that some of its data was compromised in a breach of a third-party provider. The group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility, saying it had gained access to the...

item.title: You can save $20 on the Super Mario Galaxy game bundle when you buy a Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: In celebration of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie coming to theaters, Nintendo is making it a little cheaper to get both Super Mario Galaxy games when you buy a $449.99 Nintendo Switch 2 console. Now...

item.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: At the HumanX conference, everyone was talking about Claude
item.description: Anthropic was the star of the show at San Francisco's AI-centric conference.

item.title: You can grab a refurbished 2021 Kindle Paperwhite starting at just $49.99
item.description: We spend a lot of time at The Verge waxing poetic about the latest gadgets, but sometimes it’s the last-gen devices from several years ago that offer the better value. The 2021 Kindle Paperwhite...

item.title: The Hisense UR9 is a great first shot against OLED’s bow
item.description: RGB LED TVs have been the talk of the TV world this year, with models coming from all the manufacturers, and the first one of 2026 is here - the Hisense UR9. It's the first look at the viability...

item.title: How AT&T created the most iconic phone ever
item.description: For years, even decades, virtually everyone in the United States had the same phone. Nobody really thought about it, it didn't even matter what it was called - it was just The Phone. Well, The...

item.title: The AI code wars are heating up
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the AI coding and vibe-coding booms, follow David Pierce. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: Allow me to explain why I love this camera that can’t shoot color
item.description: I love black-and-white photography. I also adore compact cameras you can always have by your side. So I'm a total mark for the Ricoh GR IV Monochrome, a fixed-lens camera that can't zoom and can't...

item.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to...

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
item.description: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier...

item.title: The Netherlands is the first European country to approve Tesla’s supervised Full Self-Driving
item.description: Dutch regulators, the RDW, announced that after over a year and a half of testing, it has officially approved Tesla's Full-Self Driving (FSD) Supervised. This makes the Netherlands the first...

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

item.title: Google says Polymarket bets showing up in News was an ‘error’
item.description: Polymarket bets started popping up in Google News alongside legitimate news articles. But now those results aren't showing, and Google says they were never supposed to. Spokesperson Ned Adriance...

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.

item.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
item.description: Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Overfishing in the South Pacific
item.description: Regulation is hard: The South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organization (SPRFMO) oversees fishing across roughly 59 million square kilometers (22 million square miles) of the South...
